mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE crosses 4.0 during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward 60-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflect capacity constraints and slowing output that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases generate positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in the majority of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in regression analysis, or 60-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product signal strong economic growth that immediately boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns during periods of positive quarterly GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing 105 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that drives corporate profit margins higher, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 in recovery regimes, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a walk-forward validation framework.
